approval_summary: Oliver approved local clean repo packaging, Vercel-readiness edits, local commit creation, and push to the specified GitHub remote after verification. Approval excludes Vercel deployment, DNS changes, WHOOP OAuth activation, secret creation, external service mutation, and force-push over unrelated history.

pages_verified.browser_heading: LifeMax remembers the small signals around your day.

no_secrets_scan.notes: Checked token, key, service-marker, environment-file path, encrypted-secret marker, private-network address, and raw payload dump indicators. The proof packet avoids echoing exact secret marker names to keep the staged scan strict.

commit_hash_note: Final pushed commit hash is recorded in the final done packet. Embedding a same-commit hash inside a tracked file is self-referential and would change the hash.
residual_risks: No Vercel deployment was performed by request.
WHOOP OAuth activation and live data connection were not performed.
The app shell is not the full LifeMax dogfood product.
npm install reported 2 moderate dependency audit findings; no forced dependency upgrades were applied.
Local shared-context preflight was red, so no wellness intensity or daily-usability claims are made.
next_actions: Import the GitHub repo into Vercel using the README settings.
Add a real public contact to the privacy policy before broad external beta access.
Configure WHOOP Developer app privacy URL after Vercel deployment is completed by Oliver.
